author: francesco-vaselli
body:
#### PR description:

Implements a new PUGenParticleProducer, which reads the CrossingFrame<HepMCProduct> from the MixingModule and produces a reco::GenParticleCollection containing all pileup particles (signal excluded) with full mother/daughter Refs, production vertex, GenStatusFlags, and collisionId() set to the bunch-crossing number. The output is in the same format as standard genParticles so it can be fed directly into, e.g., the existing GenParticlePruner.

The purpose and aims of such a producer are outlined in the recent presentation at the Simulation meeting, see [here](https://indico.cern.ch/event/1677745/#91-new-cmssw-producer-for-pile). Essentially, PU Generator Level information is deemed useful for a series of L1/HLT Phase 2 activities and for exploring novel ML workflows for analysis and simulation.

Work done with the help of @pviscone , and validated for the first time in CMSSW 14_0_9 for the production of scouting ntuples at the 2026 L1 scouting workshop (see [here](https://indi.to/HXymN))
 
@mmusich will take this forward (will tag other interested contributors)

@civanch informed as the Simulation convener during the meeting (do we want to tag CORE conveners as well?)

#### PR validation:

The PR needs extensive validation given the original development in an older release. rerunning with the basic tests (i.e. running the matrix) is advised even if no meaningful interaction is expected between those tests and the new producer. Additionally, as discussed in the meeting, we should develop simple consistency tests to make sure its product are well structured and consistent with what we would expect.

Additionally, the `struct IDto3Charge ` is copied from the `GenParticleProducer`, we might want to avoid repetition and use the same definition for both, in which case we need to handle dependencies nicely.
Finally, it would be good to discuss pruning schemas to enforce or for users to follow to avoid inflating the size of ntuples with useless PU GenParticles
title: Add PUGenParticleProducer
